Defendant Michael Coombs, Tamara Woods Notice Of Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff, A Vexatious Litigant, To Provide Security; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of K. Scott Mckay; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Michael Coombs and Tamara Woods' Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff, A Vexatious Litigant, To Provide Security is Granted. Pursuant to CCP sec. 391.1, Plaintiff must furnish security in the amount of $75,000 in order to pursue this action. The evidence submitted in support of the motion establishes that Plaintiff is a vexatious litigant and that there is not a reasonable probability that he will prevail in the litigation against Defendants. Plaintiff's argument in opposition that CCP 391.1 does not apply to represented litigants is unavailing. In Shalant v. Girardi (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1164, 1173, the case cited by Plaintiff, the Court states, "If, as here, the plaintiff is able to file the action through counsel, sections 391.1 to 391.6 continue to provide a remedy against the plaintiff's unjustifiable behavior in the pending case." Thus, even though Plaintiff is represented by counsel, CCP sec. 391.1 still applies. The Court did not consider the "Response" filed by Plaintiff on June 15, 2015, as Plaintiff was not granted leave of Court to file an additional brief. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Defendant Kevin Singer, Ed Deleski Joinder In Motion Of Defendants Coombs And Woods For Prefiling Order For Vexatious Litigant And To Require Security
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Kevin Singer and Ed Deleski's Joinder In Motion Of Defendants Coombs And Woods For Prefiling Order For Vexatious Litigant And To Require Security is Granted. Pursuant to CCP sec. 391.1, Plaintiff must furnish security in the amount of $75,000 in order to pursue this action. The evidence submitted in support of the motion establishes that Plaintiff is a vexatious litigant and that there is not a reasonable probability that he will prevail in the litigation against Defendants. Plaintiff's argument in opposition that CCP 391.1 does not apply to represented litigants is unavailing. In Shalant v. Girardi (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1164, 1173, the case cited by Plaintiff, the Court states, "If, as here, the plaintiff is able to file the action through counsel, sections 391.1 to 391.6 continue to provide a remedy against the plaintiff's unjustifiable behavior in the pending case." Thus, even though Plaintiff is represented by counsel, CCP sec. 391.1 still applies. The Court did not consider the "Response" filed by Plaintiff on June 15, 2015, as Plaintiff was not granted leave of Court to file an additional brief. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
